D10 Imaging L3 Apply Reference

Exposure Triangle — Aperture · Shutter · ISO

three knobs that all change exposure by stops; pick any two and the third is fixed by the EV target — each axis brings its own side-effect

Exposure value (EV) is a single number; three independent controls can deliver it. Aperture sets depth of field; shutter sets motion blur; ISO sets noise. Open one stop on aperture, you must compensate one stop somewhere — halve the shutter or halve the ISO. The triangle holds total exposure constant; only the side-effects change.

Drag any two axes; the third is auto-locked to preserve EV. Three mini-panels show the side-effect that axis introduces. The composite preview at the bottom shows the final result at the chosen settings.
